results.notes: Modal spell wired with resolve_modal: mode 0 creates three 1/1 colorless Hero tokens (resolve_create_token); mode 1 taps targeted creatures (TAP events) and adds a stun counter on the first (COUNTER_ADDED, counter_type='stun'). Stun counter mechanic is recognized by some engine handlers but full stun-untap interaction may still be partial.

Bespoke resolve uses create_library_search_choice with a Vehicle-subtype filter. If the spell was cast from graveyard (flashback), destination='battlefield', else 'hand'. Detects flashback via stack object's state.flashback / cast_from_graveyard flags.
Bespoke: counts caster's creatures, emits OBJECT_CREATED for one 1/2 white Moogle with lifelink per creature, then emits GRANT_KEYWORD('indestructible') for each existing creature.
Bespoke: counts (creatures controlled) + (Equipment-subtype permanents) and emits a single DAMAGE event with that amount to the targeted creature.

Partial: emits OBJECT_DESTROYED for each artifact and creature on the battlefield. The 'End the turn' clause (exile stack, discard down to max hand size, clear damage and 'until end of turn' effects) is not modeled — there is no END_THE_TURN EventType. Recommend adding an END_THE_TURN handler for full fidelity.

Bespoke: emits a COUNTER_SPELL event for each chosen target (target ability or noncreature spell). The additional-cost (sacrifice legendary creature or pay {2}) is a casting-time cost handled outside resolve.
Bespoke: emits PT_MODIFICATION(+1/+1, end_of_turn) + GRANT_KEYWORD('hexproof', end_of_turn) + UNTAP for the targeted creature you control.

Engine gap: requires opponent to make multiple choices among revealed cards (you put one in hand, then opponent puts one on bottom of YOUR library, then you take another, etc.). The engine has PendingChoice for the casting player but no scaffolded turn-passing among multiple players for sequential choices.

Engine gap: gaining control of an Equipment is unusual (most gain-control effects target creatures). The 'attach to chosen creature' part needs an ATTACH event, and the 'unattach when control is lost' rider needs a delayed trigger keyed off control changes.
Composable: resolve_draw(4). Affinity-for-Towns cost discount is a casting-cost mechanic handled outside resolve.
Bespoke: emits RETURN_FROM_GRAVEYARD + COUNTER_ADDED(+1/+1, amount=2) for the targeted creature card.
Bespoke: emits RETURN_TO_HAND_FROM_GRAVEYARD for each chosen target (up to 2 creature cards from your graveyard).
Bespoke: emits 2 OBJECT_CREATED events (tapped 2/2 black Horror tokens). If cast from graveyard (flashback flag), emits X tokens where X = creature cards in caster's graveyard.

Best-effort: emits ZONE_CHANGE for top card (library->hand) and ZONE_CHANGE for the other two (library->graveyard). Real card lets the player CHOOSE which of the 3 to keep — surfacing a PendingChoice for that selection is a future improvement.

Engine gap: 'Tiered' is a stacked-modal-cost mechanic where the player picks one of several alternative costs that determine effect strength. The chosen tier dictates base power/toughness override AND grants a delayed dies-trigger that returns the creature. Needs both Tiered cost handling and base-P/T override (different from PT_MODIFICATION).
Bespoke: PT_MODIFICATION(+3/+1) + GRANT_KEYWORD('haste') for target creature, plus IMPULSE_DRAW(amount=1, duration='end_of_next_end_step') for caster. Engine handles IMPULSE_DRAW with may-play.
Bespoke: emits DAMAGE = source's power for each OTHER creature on the battlefield. If cast from graveyard, additionally emits DISCARD for each card in caster's hand and a DRAW(4).

Engine gap: mill 4 cards, then the subset that are creature cards needs to enter the battlefield (with haste), and a delayed trigger needs to bounce them at the next end step. The shuffle+mill+conditional ETB+delayed trigger chain is an unusually complex composite.
Bespoke: reads source's power via get_power(), emits DAMAGE(amount=power, source_id=source) to both the 'any other target' and the source itself.

Engine gap: gain control + untap + haste is straightforward, but 'You may attach an Equipment you control' is an optional sub-choice, and 'unattach at next end step' is a delayed trigger. Combined with control change rules, this needs scaffolding the engine doesn't expose generically yet.

Bespoke: PT_MODIFICATION(+3/+3) + GRANT_KEYWORD('trample'). Could be expressed as resolve_chain(resolve_pump(3,3), <grant_trample>) but spell_resolve.py has no grant_keyword helper, so a small bespoke is cleanest.

Bespoke: detects kicker via stack object's state.kicked flag. Emits one DAMAGE event with amount = power (or 2*power if kicked) from source to victim. Kicker cost itself (return a land) is a casting-cost mechanic.

Engine gap: 'Choose one or more' is multi-modal selection (player can pick any subset of modes). resolve_modal supports 'choose one' but not 'choose one or more' / 'choose two' patterns. Plus the modes (fight + lore-counter add/remove on Sagas) are themselves engine gaps.

Bespoke: opens a library_search PendingChoice scoped to the top 3 cards via custom filter, with destination='hand' and shuffle_after=False. The 'put rest on bottom in any order' nuance is simplified — engine leaves them in place.

Bespoke: opens library_search PendingChoice for up to 2 basic-land or Town cards, destination='battlefield_tapped', shuffle_after=True. The 'with different names' constraint is not strictly enforced; pending-choice surface assumes player honors text.

Empty text body in this card def — Town mechanic (Affinity / lands-with-Town subtype synergy + activated face DFC) is not represented in the engine. Real card is a Town-typed sorcery DFC that turns into a Town land; needs DFC scaffolding.
Same as ISHGARD: Town DFC sorcery with empty briefing text. Engine gap: DFC + Town mechanic.
Town DFC instant face. Empty text. Engine gap: DFC + Town mechanic.
Town DFC sorcery face. Empty text. Engine gap: DFC + Town mechanic.
Town DFC sorcery face. Empty text. Engine gap: DFC + Town mechanic.
